basics.name: Fakhreddin (Dean) Emami
basics.summary: FEA Simulation Engineer focused on computational solid mechanics, high-fidelity finite element analysis, optimization, scientific machine learning, and HPC/GPU-accelerated simulation workflows.

work.summary: Led FEA, multi-physics simulation, machine learning, and experimental workflows for NASA and DARPA programs.
highlights: Executed 1M+ high-fidelity ABAQUS/UMAT/USDFLD simulations using Python and Fortran automation.
Developed scientific ML surrogates trained on 27 TB of synthetic FEA data.
Built CPU/GPU-accelerated HPC workflows with Python, CUDA/CuPy, PyTorch, and ABAQUS.

work.summary: Performed structural analysis, design, CAD documentation, quantity estimates, and shop drawing review for highway and bridge projects.
highlights: Supported HRBT and I-66 infrastructure projects in Virginia.
Worked with AASHTO, VDOT, AISC, ACI, PCA, and related design standards.

portfolio.name: FEA and ML Surrogates for Inverse Calibration
portfolio.description: Scientific ML surrogates trained on synthetic FEA datasets to accelerate inverse calibration workflows.

portfolio.name: HPC Automation for ABAQUS Simulation Campaigns
portfolio.description: CPU/GPU-accelerated Python, CUDA/CuPy, PyTorch, and ABAQUS workflows for reproducible large-scale simulation.
